scope_note: Narrow scope: only ingests docs/_research/2026-04-18_caveman-full-absorption.md and docs/_research/2026-04-18_runtime-artifact-terse-propagation.md. Older research docs are historical and reconciled as shipped via sprint-1 or superseded.

source_documents.scope_note: Doc has line-1 no-registry waiver and per-line waivers on 4 illustrative runtime calcs. No artifact-count commitments.

capabilities.title: Extend terse-output directive coverage across agents, SKILL.md gap, and shared protocols
capabilities.description: Add the /_shared/terse-output.md Additional-Resources link to 6 agent definition files (0/6 current), 6 substantive SKILL.md files currently missing it (ask, sprint, ship, next, health, todo), and 10 shared protocols. Directive is a passive reference, not a runtime instruction.
capabilities.user_value: Improves discoverability of the terse-output style for every context that loads agent definitions or shared protocols. Foundational for CAP-003 to have a target file to reference.
acceptance_criteria: 6 agent files contain 'terse-output' reference
31 SKILL.md files (25 current + 6 new) contain /_shared/terse-output.md reference
11 shared protocols cross-reference terse-output.md

capabilities.title: Wave-2 author-time compression of SAFE reference.md and research docs
capabilities.description: Run /blitz:compress on the 7 SAFE reference.md files left uncompressed after S1-005 (doc-gen, perf-profile, roadmap, completeness-gate, bootstrap, setup, fix-issue) and on 12 docs/_research/*.md files. Each pair must pass the structural validator. Estimated saving: 25-40 KB input per load.
capabilities.user_value: Reduces input-token cost of skill loading and research-doc reading across future sessions.
acceptance_criteria: 7 SAFE reference.md files have .original backups
12 research docs have .original backups
Structural validator passes for all pairs

capabilities.title: Inject runtime terse directive at skill write-sites and Agent() prompts
capabilities.description: Insert the 3-5 line inline terse-output directive into the Generate/Write phase of 8 SKILL.md files (research, sprint-plan, sprint-review, retrospective, roadmap, release, fix-issue, todo) and inject the spawn-protocol §7 OUTPUT STYLE snippet (5 lines) into the 7 UNSAFE reference.md files with agent-prompt templates. Closes the 0-to-100% propagation gap identified in research doc Finding 1.
capabilities.user_value: Runtime-generated artifacts (research docs, review reports, retrospective proposals, story bodies) stop defaulting to verbose prose. Expected 15-22% prose reduction on affected artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: 8 SKILL.md contain '**Output style:** terse-technical' block
7 UNSAFE reference.md contain 'OUTPUT STYLE: terse-technical per /_shared/terse-output.md' snippet

capabilities.title: Adopt caveman-review output format in sprint-review and review skills
capabilities.description: Rewrite sprint-review/reference.md and review/reference.md to adopt the L<line>: <severity-prefix> <problem>. <fix>. format with 🔴 (bug) / 🟡 (risk) / 🔵 (nit) / ❓ (q) prefixes and LGTM-and-stop rule. Auto-clarity (full prose) for security/CVE findings.
capabilities.user_value: Sprint-review findings become the most re-read artifact per cycle; caveman-review's format pattern is the largest single-edit leverage for readability + token reduction on reviews.
acceptance_criteria: Severity-prefix glyphs and L<line>: format present in both reference.md files
LGTM short-circuit documented
Security-finding auto-clarity exemption documented

capabilities.title: Persist output intensity and add per-section LITE exemption markers
capabilities.description: Add output_intensity: lite|full|ultra field to developer-profile.json (default lite, env override BLITZ_OUTPUT_INTENSITY). Propagate the active intensity into spawn-protocol §7 interpolation. Add LITE-intensity exemption markers to 9 safety/reasoning-sensitive skills (completeness-gate, codebase-audit, research, retrospective, sprint-review, release, migrate, fix-issue, bootstrap) naming the specific sections that stay LITE (security findings, root-cause chains, breaking-change entries, never-auto-apply rationale). Also adds the activity-feed message length soft rule to verbose-progress.md. This capability supersedes cf-task-type-gating — per-section markers are the preferred approach over whole-skill policy per Finding 6.
capabilities.user_value: Prevents brevity-induced accuracy degradation (Renze 2024, Prompt-Compression-in-the-Wild) on reasoning-heavy output sections while keeping mechanical output terse.
acceptance_criteria: developer-profile.json schema includes output_intensity
BLITZ_OUTPUT_INTENSITY env override documented in terse-output.md or spawn-protocol.md
9 skills contain LITE-intensity exemption markers
verbose-progress.md documents the ≤200-char message rule

scope_metric.note: Superseded by cf-lite-exemption-markers. Kept for registry completeness; will transition to status=dropped once CAP-005 completes.

capabilities.title: Extract agent-prompt boilerplate to shared fragment; refactor 7 UNSAFE reference.md to import
capabilities.description: Extract reused HEARTBEAT spec, PARTIAL protocol, weight-class caps, session-registration template into skills/_shared/agent-prompt-boilerplate.md. Refactor the 7 UNSAFE reference.md files to reference the shared fragment. Targets ~20-30% reduction on ~12 K tokens of per-sprint Agent() prompt input.
capabilities.user_value: Reduces redundant prompt boilerplate sent on every agent spawn. Foundational for future Agent-prompt edits to land in one place.
acceptance_criteria: skills/_shared/agent-prompt-boilerplate.md exists
7 UNSAFE reference.md files reference the shared fragment
Dumped resolved-prompt output for one test spawn matches pre-refactor byte-for-byte

capabilities.title: Upgrade spawn-protocol WARNING → BLOCKER; add sprint-review Invariant
capabilities.description: After CAP-003 lands and one full sprint ships clean, edit spawn-protocol.md:328 from WARNING (not BLOCKER) to BLOCKER and add Invariant 5 to sprint-review Phase 3.6 that fails the sprint if any Agent() prompt template omits the OUTPUT STYLE snippet.
capabilities.user_value: Prevents future regression. Without enforcement, subsequent skill edits drop the directive silently.
acceptance_criteria: spawn-protocol.md no longer contains 'WARNING (not BLOCKER)'
sprint-review Phase 3.6 Invariant 5 documented

capabilities.title: Scaffold blitz:ui-audit skill — SKILL.md, reference.md, config loader, session wiring
capabilities.description: Create skills/ui-audit/ with SKILL.md (frontmatter: model=opus, effort=low), reference.md, CHECKS.md, PATTERNS.md. Implement Phase 0 (session register + arg parse + mode routing) and Phase 1 (load browse state from docs/crawls/ or lightweight internal crawl). Load and validate .ui-audit.json config (invariants + event_invariants + role_invariants blocks). Add conflict-matrix row to session-protocol.md. Register in .claude-plugin/skill-registry.json.
capabilities.user_value: Foundational skill shell. Without this, no subsequent ui-audit capability can run. Provides the file layout, mode routing (full|smoke|data|buttons|events|consistency|heuristics|role <name>|--loop), and state-loading contract.
acceptance_criteria: skills/ui-audit/SKILL.md and reference.md exist with opus+effort:low frontmatter
skills/ui-audit/CHECKS.md and PATTERNS.md exist
.claude-plugin/skill-registry.json contains ui-audit entry with category=quality, modifies_code=false
session-protocol.md conflict matrix includes ui-audit rows
.ui-audit.json.example at repo root with all three invariant blocks
Mode routing implemented for 9 declared modes
Phase 0 + Phase 1 executable against a project with prior browse state

capabilities.title: Page data extraction + labeled-value registry (Phase 2)
capabilities.description: Implement Phase 2 extraction: per-page browser_navigate + browser_snapshot (render confirm) + browser_evaluate with label-map returning object literal. Append JSONL lines to docs/crawls/page-data-registry.jsonl with {ts, role, page, label, raw, parsed, hash, selector, tick, detail}. Role field defaults __default__ for backward compat. Hash raw for tick-diff. 1-line schema addition to browse/reference.md exposing page_data_registry path in latest-tick.json.
capabilities.user_value: Turns every page visit into labeled semantic data that downstream invariants can reason about. Ground truth via DOM .textContent avoids a11y-tree truncation.
acceptance_criteria: Extraction phase runs browser_evaluate with user-declared label map
One JSONL line per (role, page, label) observation written to docs/crawls/page-data-registry.jsonl
Each line includes raw, parsed (Number coerced), hash, selector, tick, role
browse/reference.md latest-tick.json schema includes page_data_registry field
Integration test: 3 pages × 2 labels = 6 registry lines

capabilities.title: Consistency check + invariant evaluator + flapping/stale detection
capabilities.description: Implement Phase 3: reduce page-data-registry.jsonl via jq group_by(.label); flag cross-page mismatches unless covered by declared invariant with tolerance. Evaluate .ui-audit.json invariants (checks: equal, gte, lte). Tick-over-tick hash diff → STABLE/CHANGED/STALE/FLAPPING/NULL_TRANSITION taxonomy. Emit invariant_fail events to activity feed.
capabilities.user_value: The core 'dashboard says 47 but list page says 46' detector. Fills the gap no visual-regression tool addresses.
acceptance_criteria: jq reducer groups by label, detects cross-page divergence
equal/gte/lte invariant checks evaluate correctly
Tolerance honored on numeric checks
FLAPPING detected after ≥3 ticks of oscillation
STALE detected on revert within 2 ticks
invariant_fail events written to .cc-sessions/activity-feed.jsonl
Test: seeded registry with known divergence produces expected findings

capabilities.title: Data-quality flags — null, placeholder, format, stale-zero, broken-total, negative-count
capabilities.description: Phase 4 quality checks. In-extraction JS applies regex for placeholders, null detection, negative counts, format-mismatch against prior observations for same label. Broken-total computed from declared parent/child label relationships in config. Stale-zero compares to registry history.
capabilities.user_value: Catches data corruption early — a bug that makes a value render as null or zero is flagged, not silently passed.
acceptance_criteria: 6 quality flags implemented: NULL_VALUE, PLACEHOLDER, FORMAT_MISMATCH, STALE_ZERO, BROKEN_TOTAL, NEGATIVE_COUNT
Placeholder regex configurable in .ui-audit.json
Broken-total requires parent/child label declaration in config
Findings include page:label:flag format

capabilities.title: UI/UX heuristic audit — Vercel rules + severity tiers + a11y
capabilities.description: Phase 5: load Vercel Web Interface Guidelines from upstream URL (or vendored copy in PATTERNS.md) and evaluate categories 9 (Nav+State) and 16 (Content+Copy, tabular-nums) against DOM snapshots. Apply UX Pro Max severity ladder CRITICAL/HIGH/MED/LOW. Parallelize via sonnet Agent workers when page count >30.
capabilities.user_value: Catches Vercel-guideline violations and a11y issues alongside data checks — single audit pass covers data + design.
acceptance_criteria: Vercel rules source vendored in PATTERNS.md with upstream URL comment
Categories 9 and 16 evaluation implemented
Severity tiering CRITICAL/HIGH/MED/LOW applied to findings
Spawned sonnet workers pass model: sonnet explicitly
Inline execution when page count ≤30; parallel when >30

capabilities.title: Audit reporter — markdown report + stdout summary + activity-feed log
capabilities.description: Phase 6: write docs/crawls/ui-audit-report.md with findings grouped by severity, file:line for code-citable, page:label for registry findings. Stdout summary: counts by severity + top 3 invariant failures. Append skill_complete event to activity-feed with finding counts.
capabilities.user_value: Makes findings actionable. Without a report, registry drift is invisible.
acceptance_criteria: docs/crawls/ui-audit-report.md written on every run
Findings grouped by severity tier
Stdout summary includes counts + top 3 invariant failures
skill_complete event appended to .cc-sessions/activity-feed.jsonl with detail block

capabilities.title: Interactive element coverage — every button/link/tab enumerated + audited
capabilities.description: Enumerate every ARIA-role + native interactive element per page via browser_evaluate. Per-element checks: NO_LABEL, DEAD_HREF, EMPTY_HANDLER, TABINDEX_POSITIVE, TABINDEX_NEGATIVE_VISIBLE, NO_FOCUS_STATE. Safe-click classifier filters destructive labels/hrefs before any browser_click. Per-page coverage summary line written to registry.
capabilities.user_value: Catches unlabeled buttons, dead links, missing keyboard reachability — the long tail of a11y + UX bugs structural checks miss.
acceptance_criteria: browser_evaluate enumeration returns all interactive elements with {tag, role, label, tabindex, hrefOrOnclick, outerSnip}
6 per-element checks produce findings in page:button_label:issue format
Destructive-click classifier gates safe-click pass
TABINDEX_MISSING check re-runs after 500ms settle window (R7 mitigation)
Per-page interactive_audit_summary line appended to registry

capabilities.title: Analytics event consistency — dataLayer + sendBeacon + network interception
capabilities.description: Three-layer event interception: window.dataLayer push proxy + navigator.sendBeacon wrap + network-level filter (Segment/PostHog/Amplitude/GA4). Drain __auditEventLog after each action. Registry label=analytics_event, keyed (page, action_trigger) → {event_name, props}. Cross-page drift via jq group_by(.detail.event_name) + props_hash compare. .ui-audit.json event_invariants block: required_props, forbidden_props, scope.
capabilities.user_value: Catches 'same user action fires different event schemas on different pages' — a class of analytics bugs that ship to prod and silently corrupt dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: dataLayer proxy + sendBeacon wrap installed immediately after browser_navigate
Network-level filter captures Segment/PostHog/Amplitude/GA4 hosts
Event log drained after each action; registry lines written with event_name, layer, props
Cross-page drift detection via jq
event_invariants with required_props/forbidden_props/scope evaluated
EVENTS_BEFORE_SPY flag when dataLayer non-empty at inject time (R8 mitigation)

capabilities.title: Per-permissions-role audit matrix — 5-role harness + role invariants + leak detection
capabilities.description: Multi-role audit: anonymous + viewer + member + admin + superadmin via env-var-provisioned test users. storageState pattern adapted for Playwright MCP (harvest via browser_evaluate, restore via replay login or localStorage injection). Registry role field namespaces observations. .ui-audit.json role_invariants: equal, viewer_null, gte checks. HTML source scan for role-leak patterns. Loop cycles (role, page) pairs. ETA warning + --yes/--ci gating on full matrix runs.
capabilities.user_value: Catches privilege-boundary bugs (admin data leaking to viewer), role-specific UX regressions, and per-role data divergence. No mainstream tool does this.
acceptance_criteria: 5 roles configured via AUDIT_<ROLE>_EMAIL/_PASS env vars; skip-if-absent
Scripted login flow per role with storageState harvest + write to .auth/<role>.json
Sentinel check after role switch (R9 mitigation)
Registry role field present; __default__ backward compat preserved
role_invariants checks: equal, viewer_null, gte — all evaluate correctly
Role-leak scan with configurable patterns in role_leak_patterns
latest-tick.json extended: mode, current_role, current_page_idx, roles_complete, roles_pending
ETA warning printed upfront; --yes/--ci gate on >60 min runs (R10 mitigation)

dedup_log.reason: Per docs/_research/2026-04-18_runtime-artifact-terse-propagation.md Finding 6, per-section LITE markers (9 skills) supersede the whole-skill output_style_policy approach (5 skills). Task-type-gating registry entry retained and will transition to status=dropped once CAP-005 completes; acceptance criteria overlap and lite-exemption is the stronger contract.
